title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: hypnoR hypnogram layer Circadia Lab R ecosystem. accepts staged hypnograms two upstream sources: mrpheus — full AASM staging: W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM zeitR — coarse actigraphy-derived staging: W / Sleep / Quiet sleep metric functions resolution-agnostic: compute possible given available stages document outputs require full AASM staging.

title: Getting started with hypnoR
code: library(hypnoR)  # 1. Read a hypnogram hyp <- read_hypnogram("path/to/hypnogram.csv")  # 2. Compute sleep architecture metrics arch <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp)  # 3. Detect NREM/REM cycles (full AASM only) cyc <- compute_cycles(hyp)  # 4. Stage-transition analysis trans <- compute_transitions(hyp)  # 5. Plot plot_hypnogram(hyp) plot_architecture(arch) plot_transition_matrix(trans$matrix)

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: read_hypnogram() currently handles: Pass format = "auto" (default) infer format file header extension.

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: hypnoR detects resolution automatically stage labels present hypnogram. Metrics require full AASM staging (REM latency, SWS latency, cycle detection, N1/N2/N3 percentages) return NA informative message coarse hypnograms.

what: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: Hypnogram handling, plotting, sleep architecture metrics R — staging-agnostic layer Circadia Lab ecosystem.

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: hypnoR hypnogram layer Circadia Lab R ecosystem. sits raw signal sources integrated participant database, providing common interface sleep staging regardless staging came . accepts two staging resolutions: Full AASM (5-state: W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM) — supplied mrpheus Coarse (3-state: W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) — supplied zeitR metric functions resolution-agnostic: compute every metric possible given available stages return NA informative message metrics require full AASM staging. Downstream, hypnoR feeds syncR::sync() part unified participant-indexed database.

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: Flexible ingestion — read_hypnogram() reads EDF annotations, CSV, YASA output, Compumedics Profusion, Nox Medical formats. Architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture() returns TST, SE, SOL, WASO, REM latency, SWS latency, stage percentages; metrics degrade gracefully coarse hypnograms. Cycle segmentation — compute_cycles() detects NREM/REM cycles via Feinberg & Floyd (1979) AASM rules. Transition analysis — compute_transitions() builds stage--stage transition probability matrix computes fragmentation index. Publication-ready plots — plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), plot_transition_matrix() use theme_circadia() circadia shared visual identity package.

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
code: hypnoR/ ├── R/ │   ├── hypnoR-package.R       # package-level docs │   ├── read_hypnogram.R       # ingestion │   ├── architecture.R         # compute_sleep_architecture() │   ├── cycles.R               # compute_cycles() │   ├── transitions.R          # compute_transitions() │   ├── plot.R                 # plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), │   │                          #   plot_transition_matrix() │   └── utils.R                # internal helpers ├── tests/testthat/ ├── vignettes/ │   └── getting-started.Rmd ├── man/figures/               # logo, favicon, card ├── .github/workflows/         # R CMD CHECK + pkgdown CI ├── _pkgdown.yml └── DESCRIPTION

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: Prerequisites: R ≥ 4.1, remotes. Basic workflow:
code: remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/hypnoR") library(hypnoR)  hyp  <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") arch <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp) cyc  <- compute_cycles(hyp)           # full AASM only trans <- compute_transitions(hyp)  plot_hypnogram(hyp) plot_architecture(arch) plot_transition_matrix(trans$matrix)

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: Circadia Lab, Northumbria University.

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: 📦 zeitR — wrist actigraphy analysis circadian metrics; upstream source coarse hypnograms 📦 mrpheus — PSG signal analysis; upstream source full AASM hypnograms 📦 syncR — integrates zeitR, slumbR, tallieR, hypnoR unified participant-indexed database 📦 circadia — shared visual identity: palettes, themes, scales 🔬 circadia-bio — Circadia Lab GitHub organisation

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: Segments hypnogram NREM/REM cycles using either Feinberg & Floyd (1979) rules simplified AASM-compatible algorithm.  applicable full AASM hypnograms; returns informative error coarse actigraphy-derived hypnograms.

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: hypnogram tibble returned read_hypnogram(). method "feinberg_floyd" (default) "aasm". epoch_sec Epoch duration seconds.  Default 30. min_rem_epochs Minimum number consecutive REM epochs qualify REM period.  Default 5 (2.5 min 30-s epochs).

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: tibble one row per detected cycle: cycle Integer cycle index. start_epoch,end_epoch First last epoch cycle. nrem_min,rem_min Duration NREM REM portions (minutes). cycle_min Total cycle duration (minutes).

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: Feinberg, ., & Floyd, T. C. (1979). Systematic trends across night human sleep cycles. Psychophysiology, 16(3), 283–291.

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
text: Derives standard sleep architecture summary statistics staged hypnogram.  metrics resolution-agnostic: function computes every metric possible given available staging levels silently omits metrics require stages present hypnogram (e.g. REM latency requires full AASM hypnogram).

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
code: compute_sleep_architecture(   hypnogram,   epoch_sec = 30L,   lights_off = NULL,   lights_on = NULL )

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
text: hypnogram tibble returned read_hypnogram(), tibble minimum columns epoch (integer) stage (factor). epoch_sec Epoch duration seconds.  Default 30. lights_off, lights_on Optional POSIXct timestamps lights-lights-.  supplied, TIB (time bed) SE (sleep efficiency) computed relative recording period; otherwise first last sleep epoch bound period.

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
text: one-row tibble columns: tst_min Total sleep time (minutes). tib_min Time bed (minutes).  NA lights_off/lights_on supplied. se_pct Sleep efficiency (percent).  NA TIB unavailable. sol_min Sleep onset latency (minutes). waso_min Wake sleep onset (minutes). rem_lat_min REM latency (minutes).  NA coarse hypnograms. sws_lat_min SWS (N3) latency (minutes).  NA coarse hypnograms. pct_n1,pct_n2,pct_n3,pct_rem Stage percentages TST.  NA stages absent hypnogram. pct_sleep,pct_quiet_sleep Stage percentages coarse hypnograms.  NA full AASM hypnograms. staging_resolution "aasm" "coarse".

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
text: Builds stage--stage transition probability matrix derives fragmentation indices staged hypnogram.  Works full AASM coarse actigraphy-derived staging.

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
text: hypnogram tibble returned read_hypnogram(). normalise TRUE (default), row transition count matrix divided row sum give transition probabilities. FALSE, raw transition counts returned.

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
text: list two elements: matrix square tibble (stages × stages) transition probabilities counts.  Row = stage, column = stage. fragmentation one-row tibble : n_transitions Total number stage transitions. fragmentation_index Proportion epochs followed different stage. wake_transitions Number transitions Wake (proxy arousal burden).

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp  <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") trans <- compute_transitions(hyp) trans$matrix trans$fragmentation } # }

what: hypnoR: Hypnogram Handling, Plotting, and Sleep Architecture Metrics — hypnoR-package
title: hypnoR: Hypnogram Handling, Plotting, and Sleep Architecture Metrics — hypnoR-package
text: Provides staging-agnostic layer hypnogram ingestion, sleep architecture metric computation, cycle segmentation, transition analysis. Accepts full AASM-staged hypnograms (W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM) mrpheus coarser actigraphy-derived hypnograms (W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) zeitR; metric functions degrade gracefully depending staging resolution. Includes publication-ready plotting via theme_circadia(). Designed hypnogram layer Circadia Lab ecosystem, feeding syncR::sync().

title: hypnoR: Hypnogram Handling, Plotting, and Sleep Architecture Metrics — hypnoR-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
text: Renders stage durations percentages horizontal bar chart using ggplot2 theme_circadia().

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
code: plot_architecture(   architecture,   metric = "duration",   colours = NULL,   title = NULL )

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
text: architecture one-row tibble returned compute_sleep_architecture(), multi-row tibble comparing multiple nights (requires night id grouping column). metric "duration" (minutes, default) "percentage" TST. colours Named character vector stage colours.  See plot_hypnogram() defaults. title Optional plot title.

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp  <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") arch <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp) plot_architecture(arch) } # }

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
text: Renders classic stage--time hypnogram using ggplot2 theme_circadia().  Accepts full AASM coarse staging; y-axis order colour mapping set automatically staging resolution detected hypnogram.

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
code: plot_hypnogram(   hypnogram,   epoch_sec = 30L,   colours = NULL,   show_cycles = FALSE,   title = NULL )

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
text: hypnogram tibble returned read_hypnogram(). epoch_sec Epoch duration seconds, used construct time axis.  Default 30. colours Named character vector mapping stage labels hex colours. Defaults Circadia Lab palette via circadia::domain_colour_for(), available, otherwise built-fallback. show_cycles TRUE cycle information available (cycle column present hypnogram), cycle boundaries overlaid vertical dashed lines.  Default FALSE. title Optional plot title.

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
text: Renders transition probability (count) matrix returned compute_transitions() heatmap using ggplot2 theme_circadia().

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
code: plot_transition_matrix(   transitions,   label_values = TRUE,   digits = 2L,   title = NULL )

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
text: transitions matrix element list returned compute_transitions(). label_values TRUE (default), cell values printed inside tile. digits Number decimal places cell labels.  Default 2. title Optional plot title.

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp   <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") trans <- compute_transitions(hyp) plot_transition_matrix(trans$matrix) } # }

what: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: Imports staged hypnogram common formats returns tidy tibble one row per epoch.  resulting object standard input hypnoR functions.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
code: read_hypnogram(   path,   format = "auto",   epoch_sec = 30L,   tz = "UTC",   quiet = FALSE )

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: path Path hypnogram file. format One "csv", "edf_annotations", "yasa", "compumedics", "nox", "auto" (default).  "auto", format inferred file extension header. epoch_sec Epoch duration seconds.  Default 30. tz Time zone string passed lubridate::as_datetime(). Default "UTC". quiet Suppress informational messages.  Default FALSE.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: tibble columns: epoch Integer epoch index (1-based). time POSIXct timestamp start epoch. stage Ordered factor.  Level set depends staging resolution: full AASM (W, N1, N2, N3, REM) coarse actigraphy-derived (W, Sleep, Quiet sleep). source Character — originating device / scorer label, available file header.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: hypnoR distinguishes two staging resolutions: Full AASM (5-state): supplied mrpheus. Coarse (3-state): supplied zeitR. downstream metric functions resolution-agnostic: compute possible given available stages document metrics require full AASM staging.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") hyp <- read_hypnogram("night_001.edf", format = "edf_annotations") } # }

title: hypnoR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: Package skeleton: read_hypnogram(), compute_sleep_architecture(), compute_cycles(), compute_transitions(), plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), plot_transition_matrix(). Staging-agnostic design: metric functions accept full AASM (W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM) coarse actigraphy-derived (W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) hypnograms. pkgdown site Bootstrap 5 Circadia Lab branding.
